channel.title: Client Certificates on Kovasky Buezo | Software Developer, Cybersecurity Enthusiast
channel.description: Recent content in Client Certificates on Kovasky Buezo | Software Developer, Cybersecurity Enthusiast

item.title: Configure pfSense HAProxy mTLS (Client Certificate Authentication) for specific subdomains
item.description: Intro I’m hosting ERPNext and Nextcloud for my family. Since these services contain more sensitive data, I wanted an extra layer of protection that limits access to approved devices without having to manage a VPN or keep track of a dynamic IP whitelist. The solution I landed on was mTLS (client certificate authentication), where the reverse proxy requests the client to present a valid certificate signed by an approved CA. This post shows exactly how I set it up on pfSense 2.
